author: śrīla raghunātha bhāgabatācārya
verses:
stutiyogya tumi prabhu, navaghana śyāma
vijurī ujjvala⋅pītavastra⋅paridhāma

navaguñjā⋅avataṁsa śravaṇa⋅bhūṣaṇa
śikhaṇḍa⋅maṇḍita keśa prasanna⋅vadana

ājānulambita vanamālā vilolita
veṇu, vetra, viṣāṇa, kabala virājita

amala⋅kamala jini’ caraṇasundara
namo namo nandagopa⋅suta manohara

kṛṣṇa, kṛṣṇa vṛṣṇikula⋅puṣkara⋅bhāṣkara
pṛthvī⋅deva⋅dvija⋅paśu⋅sindhu⋅śaśadhara

uddharma⋅śārvara⋅hara asura⋅saŋhārī
arka⋅ādi⋅sarvasura⋅pujya adhikārī

ākalparyanta mora rahu namaskāra
ei vara māgo, nātha, caraṇe tomāra

tomāra padāravinda sevo nirantara
ei ājñā kara more karuṇāsāgara

(śrī⋅kṛṣṇaprema⋅taraṅgiṇī)
en_translation:
You are worthy of praise, O Lord! Dark like a fresh cloud. Dressed in yellow garments bright like lightning.

Ornaments of fresh Guñjā decorating Your ears. Hair adorned with peacock feathers, joyful face.

A forest garland hanging down to Your knees, swinging. Resplendent with flute, stick, horn, and morsel.

Beautiful feet surpassing the spotless lotus. Obeisances, obeisances to the enchanting son of Nanda Gopa.

Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa! Sun of the lotus of the Vṛṣṇi dynasty. Moon of the ocean of the earth, gods, brāhmaṇas, and cattle.

Destroyer of the night of irreligion, slayer of the demons. The Authority worshipable by the Sun and all the gods.

Let my obeisances remain until the end of the kalpa. I beg this boon, O Lord, at Your feet.

May I serve Your lotus feet constantly. Give me this order, O Ocean of Mercy.

(Excerpt from Śrī Kṛṣṇa-prema-taraṅgiṇī)
